mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in the unemployment rate below 4% signals tightening labor markets, which correlates with stronger corporate earnings growth and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in 60%+ of instances where UNRATE falls below 4%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in the federal funds rate and core inflation signal a hawkish monetary policy stance that raises equity discount rates, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in 60%+ of instances where both FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL rise.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in industrial production above 105 signals expanding manufacturing activity, which drives corporate profits and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in 60%+ of instances where INDPRO exceeds 105.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in housing starts above 1.3 million units signals stronger residential construction activity, which correlates with positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in 60%+ of instances where HOUST exceeds 1300.

mechanism_narrative: During monetary tightening cycles, rising federal funds rates above 2% signal higher borrowing costs that suppress equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in 60%+ of instances where FEDFUNDS exceeds 2% during tightening regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth above $17.385 trillion signals strong macroeconomic demand, which drives corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60d returns in 60%+ of instances where GDPC1 exceeds $17.385 trillion.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in payrolls and declining unemployment signals a robust labor market, which drives consumer spending and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in 60%+ of instances where PAYEMS exceeds 148,424 and UNRATE falls below 3.9.
